free_text: Fast neutron fission of 241Am
Central Laboratory,McClellan AFB,California
Irradiations were made using FLATTOP
critical assembly of the Los Alamos Nat. Laboratory.
FLATTOP is natural uranium reflected 239Pu
Ga. The assembly has a neutron output capable of
producing approx. 10E+12 fissions/g*h in 235U target
material
Each target consisted of 2.3 to 7.5 mg of 241Am as
Am(NO3)3 cold welded between two sheets 0.025 mm Al
foil. Target consisted of an 241Am target either
alone or sandwiched between monitor foils
encapsulated in Al. Pair of either 235U
238U(238U-99.8%,235U-0.2%). Fission catcher foil
electroplated targets contained about 100mug/cm2 of
241Am within diameter of 9.5mm on 0.025mm thick Pt
discs.
Catcher foils measurements were made directly by
Ge(Li) gamma ray detector. 241Am targets were first
separated from to achieve complete 241Am removal
Radiochemical separation and
catcher foil techniques were used. Activity was
determined either by Ge(Li) spectrometry or by
beta-counting techniques.
Neutron energy at target position is somewhat degraded
from the fission spectrum
Statistical uncertainty
Systematic uncertainty
Compiled by S.H.
SD: Small correction in SAMPLE was made.
OS. Minor BIB correction
